The Vault v0.4.8 catches the Windows installer release up with the current local memory OS.

Highlights:
- Adds the optional Vault Collab cockpit for live multi-agent coordination with handoff inboxes, role-grouped sessions, policy visibility, and append-only discussion context.
- Adds permission-gated Vault Collab launch requests so new workers require explicit approval and expose a copyable command fallback.
- Adds one-click client setup improvements for connecting Codex, Claude Desktop, Claude Code, and the optional Vault Collab MCP server.
- Adds exhaustive open-loop audit MCP tools and keeps recall responses focused on the most urgent close-the-loop prompts.
- Improves the Projects surface with searchable project rows, workspace signals, momentum, and reviewable project deletion.
- Hardens Graphify integration, including graph-aware recall, runtime/artifact handling, telemetry, and Windows-safe build behavior.
- Caps oversized memory save/update content before persistence and adds regression coverage around portable native dependency extraction.
- Removes the experimental Spark voice extension from the desktop release line to keep this installer focused on stable Vault, Graphify, and Vault Collab workflows.
- Refreshes the README, screenshots, and current feature documentation for the installer-era desktop app.

This build packages the desktop app and the bundled `vault-memory` MCP sidecar runtime. After installing, open The Vault and use Settings -> Client setup to connect Codex, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code.
